framework: Claude for Financial Services — equity-research methodology (/screen, /sector, /comps, /catalysts, /thesis). Data: Robinhood fundamentals + web_search (no paid-vendor MCP connectors).
action: Weekend run (all orders queue for the 7/20 open): rotate the sleeve to match a regime that is selling tech beats — exit TSM and NFLX (both beat the exact metrics the 7/15 thesis named, and both got sold anyway), recycle into energy (CVX 5→10, new XOM 6), nudge LLY 6→8, hold BAC/REGN/META, hold PYPL as merger-arb into Monday's board meeting, hold the GOOGL 5% starter into the 7/22 print.
sizing: 63% deployed across 8 names (BAC 10, REGN 10, CVX 10, META 8, LLY 8, PYPL 6, XOM 6, GOOGL 5), 37% cash; the three buys are limit-capped ~1% above the 7/17 close so nothing chases Monday's open, exits are market-on-open

regime.label: Hawkish-Fed, hot-oil, sell-the-beat tape — semis routed on a Chinese AI-model capex scare; energy is the only sleeve making new highs
regime.note: The disinflation-relief regime of 7/15 broke in 48 hours. Friday 7/17: S&P -1.0%, Nasdaq -1.5% (Nasdaq -2.9% on the week), a second straight session of semiconductor rout after Chinese startup Moonshot AI unveiled a model it says narrows the gap with top US offerings — reviving AI-capex-scale-back fears that sold even TSMC's blowout beat. The Fed under new Chair Kevin Warsh is signaling potential rate HIKES this fall with inflation at a three-year-high 4.2%, oil is bid on renewed Middle East escalation (WTI ~$79.56, Brent ~$84.95), and Trump's claim that China interfered in the 2020 election put the trade truce back in question. Three data points define the tape: ASML's raise sold on 7/15, TSM's beat sold on 7/16, NFLX's margin beat sold to a fresh 52-wk low on 7/17. This market is not paying for good tech news; it is paying for energy (CVX/XOM/COP/FANG all closed above their 20-day highs Friday) and rate-lever financials (BAC printed a fresh 52-wk high 62.115 Friday, in a -1% tape).

thesis: What changed since 7/15: both of the book's binary prints resolved, both BEAT the exact metric the prior thesis named, and both positions lost money anyway — that is a regime verdict, not an underwriting miss, and the book now trades the regime. TSM printed Q2 revenue of $40.20B against the $39.3B consensus the thesis named, with gross margin 67.7% above the top of guidance and net income +77% y/y — and the stock fell from 419 to 398 in two sessions as the Moonshot AI capex scare routed the whole semi complex (SOXL -42% from its 20-day high). NFLX beat the named 32.6% operating-margin guide at 33.4% and beat EPS ($0.80 vs $0.79) — and fell 7.3% to a fresh 52-week low ($65.08) on the H2 revenue-deceleration guide and a $1.53B FCF quarter against $2.72B expected. Both exit at Monday's open: the underwriting discipline held (name a metric that prints, bet on it printing better), but a tape that sells beats leaves an event book with no way to get paid, and neither name has another dated catalyst inside ~15 ticks (TSM next prints October). The proceeds rotate to where the tape IS paying: energy. CVX doubles 5→10 and XOM starts at 6 — CVX/XOM/COP/FANG all closed Friday above their 20-day highs while the S&P fell 1%, WTI sits ~$79.56 on renewed Middle East escalation, the Warsh Fed's hawkish tilt (4.2% inflation, possible fall hikes) makes energy the natural inflation hedge, and both carry real yield support (CVX 3.80%, XOM 2.80%) with XOM's 7/31 print ~9 ticks out as the sleeve's next dated event; XOM's Friday volume ran 19.0M vs a 15.2M two-week average — the breakout is participated, not drifting. The event sleeve keeps its two live, non-earnings catalysts: PYPL (6) holds into Monday's board meeting on the $60.50 Stripe/Advent bid — at 56.56 the spread to the bid is +7.0%, the position is +21% from its 46.72 entry, and at 10.6x earnings the standalone floor limits deal-break damage; no add at RSI 81, no trim the session before the decision meeting. GOOGL (5) holds into Wednesday's print: consensus $2.90 (+25.5%) on $116.9B revenue, and unlike ASML/NFLX into their prints, GOOGL enters -15% off its May high at RSI 42 in the cheapest Mag-7 valuation band (27x) — the one earnings bet where the crowd is positioned OUT, which is the only kind this tape has paid (PYPL was bought before the bid, not after). BAC (10) holds — fresh 52-wk high Friday at 14.1x with the hawkish Fed as a NII tailwind; REGN (10) holds as the valuation-floor sleeve (+11.3% over 20 sessions at 16.5x); LLY nudges 6→8 (+6.0% over 20 sessions in a flat tape, ~Aug 6 print ~13 ticks out); META (8) holds into its ~7/29 print at 24.2x, the second-cheapest mega-cap. Deployment lands at 63%, confidence 0.55: the 37% cash is a deliberate regime buffer — a market selling beats with a Fed threatening hikes is not one to be fully invested in, and the cash re-enters after GOOGL's print and the PYPL board verdict resolve this week's two live events.

evidence.point: TSM Q2 2026 (reported 7/16 pre-open): 'revenue was $40.20 billion, which increased 33.7% year-over-year'; 'net income and diluted EPS both increased 77.4%'; 'Gross margin came in at 67.7%, above the guided range of 65.5% to 67.5%' — vs the $39.3B consensus the 7/15 thesis named. Stock reaction: 'muted to negative', then 419.48 → 409.74 (7/16) → 398.37 (7/17) into the sector rout

NFLX Q2 2026 (reported 7/16 after close): 'revenue of $12.56 billion, up 13% year over year'; 'earnings of 80 cents per share, a penny ahead of the 79-cent consensus'; 'Operating margin was 33.4%, which exceeded the company's Q2 guidance' (the 32.6% guide the 7/15 thesis named) — but 'free cash flow fell 33% to $1.53 billion, well below the $2.72 billion expected' and 'Netflix sank 7.3% after forecasting another quarter of slowing sales', closing 68.95 with a fresh 52-wk low of 65.08 set 7/17

Friday 7/17 tape: 'The S&P 500 lost 1%, the Nasdaq shed 1.5%, and the Dow Jones retreated 407 points'; 'semiconductors were hit especially hard after Chinese startup Moonshot AI unveiled a new model that it said narrows the gap with the top offerings in the U.S.'; 'the Nasdaq slid 2.9% for the week'

PYPL bid status: Stripe/Advent 'offer of $60.50 a share, a 28% premium', deal 'valued at approximately $53.4 billion' with 'roughly $50 billion in committed bank financing'; 'PayPal's board will meet as soon as July 20 to discuss the offer'; PayPal 'working with Goldman, Evercore'. PYPL closed 56.56 (7/17) — a +7.0% spread to the bid; entry was 46.72 on 7/14

Macro regime: 'The Federal Reserve... has pivoted to a more hawkish stance under new Chair Kevin Warsh. The central bank is now signaling potential rate hikes this fall as inflation reached a three-year high of 4.2% in May'; oil bid on Middle East escalation — 'West Texas Intermediate futures rose 1.82% to $79.56 per barrel, with Brent futures up 1.98% to $84.95'

GOOGL into 7/22 (after close): 'Analysts expect Alphabet to report Q2 earnings of $2.90 per share, up 25.5% year over year, on revenue of $116.9 billion (+21.3% YoY)'; stock closed 346.77 — 15.1% below its 408.61 52-wk high (5/18) at 27.0x earnings, RSI 42

Energy breakout, Friday 7/17 closes vs prior 20-day highs: CVX 187.38 (+1.4% above), XOM 147.36 (+0.3%), FANG 195.54 (+0.9%), COP 114.71 (+0.8%) — the only universe sleeve above its 20-day highs in a -1% S&P session; XOM volume 19.03M vs 15.16M two-week average; CVX dividend yield 3.80% at 31.9x, XOM 2.80% at 24.6x
BAC: fresh 52-wk high 62.115 set Friday 2026-07-17 (a -1% S&P session), closed 61.27 at 14.1x earnings, 1.56x book; RSI 70
PYPL valuation floor: 10.64x trailing earnings at 56.56 (the bid at 60.50 ≈ 11.4x); REGN 16.5x, 2.27x book; META 24.2x; LLY 42.1x with the stock +6.0% over 20 sessions vs SPY +0.3%

risks: PYPL board rejects the bid outright Monday with no raised offer — the stock gives back much of the +21% gain toward the ~47 pre-bid level; the 10.6x standalone multiple and the revealed strategic interest are the floor, but the mark-to-market hit lands the same session the book reports

GOOGL joins the sell-the-beat pattern 7/22 — ASML, TSM and NFLX all beat and sold; the position is sized at 5% precisely because the pattern has hit three for three, and a fourth instance exits the earnings-bet business for this regime entirely

Middle East de-escalates suddenly — the oil bid unwinds and the fresh 16% energy sleeve (CVX+XOM) gives back its breakout the way the July 6 Iran-de-escalation leg did; the 3.8%/2.8% yields cushion but don't prevent that

The Moonshot AI capex scare proves overdone and semis V-recover — the TSM exit at Monday's open realizes a -13% loss at the local low; accepted: the position had no dated catalyst until October to underwrite a recovery against

A hawkish-Fed hike surprise this fall compresses all equity multiples — the 37% cash buffer and the two sub-17x names (BAC, REGN) are the only real defense; deployment stays capped until the regime pays for more

web_search: Friday 7/17 tape recap, NFLX Q2 results, TSM Q2 results, PYPL bid status, week 7/20–24 earnings calendar, Fed/oil regime
data_examined.source: Yahoo Finance, TheStreet, qz.com, Alphastreet, SEC 6-K, TechCrunch, Bloomberg, Axios, Kiplinger, intellectia.ai

data_examined.label: Prior book, realized P&L, alpha vs SPY and reflection

rationale.BAC: Hold 10. [DATA] Fresh 52-wk high 62.115 printed Friday 7/17 — in a -1% S&P session — at 14.1x earnings and 1.56x book (RH fundamentals); +8.4% over 20 sessions vs SPY +0.3%. [ASSUMED] A Warsh Fed threatening hikes is a net-interest-income tailwind, making BAC the rare name whose regime exposure and momentum point the same way. RSI 70 says extended, so it holds rather than adds.
rationale.REGN: Hold 10. [DATA] +11.3% over 20 sessions at 16.5x earnings and 2.27x book, closed within 0.5% of its 20-day high (screen + RH fundamentals). [ASSUMED] The valuation-floor sleeve keeps earning its seat in a multiple-compression regime — cheap, working, and uncorrelated to the AI-capex story that is wrecking the tape's growth wing.
rationale.CVX: Add 5→10, limit 189.30 (~1% above the 187.38 close). [DATA] Closed 1.4% above its prior 20-day high Friday; WTI ~$79.56 on renewed Middle East escalation; 3.80% dividend yield; the CVX/XOM/COP/FANG group was the only universe sleeve above its 20-day highs in Friday's -1% tape. [ASSUMED] With 4.2% inflation and a Fed signaling hikes, energy is the regime's natural long — this is the tape paying somewhere, and the book goes where it pays. The 7/14 entry at 182.84 is +2.5%; doubling on the breakout rather than the dip is deliberate: in this sleeve the trend is the thesis.
rationale.META: Hold 8. [DATA] 24.2x earnings — second-cheapest mega-cap after the routed GOOGL — still +13.8% over 20 sessions; reports ~7/29 (~7 ticks out). [ASSUMED] The hold-through-print decision gets made next tick with the GOOGL result in hand; if Wednesday shows even de-rated mega-cap beats getting sold, META's print exposure gets cut before 7/29.
rationale.LLY: Nudge 6→8, limit 1190.00 (~1% above the 1179.11 close). [DATA] +6.0% over 20 sessions vs SPY +0.3%, recovered to -3.4% vs the 1220.75 entry; ~Aug 6 print is ~13 ticks out (prior calendar pull). [ASSUMED] GLP-1 duopoly demand is rate-insensitive and the relative strength through a hostile fortnight says the market agrees; 42x is the sleeve's one expensive multiple, which caps the add at 2 points.
rationale.PYPL: Hold 6 — merger-arb, event tomorrow. [DATA] Board meets 'as soon as July 20' on the $60.50 Stripe/Advent bid ($53.4B, ~$50B committed financing); closed 56.56 = +7.0% spread to the bid, +21% on the 46.72 entry; 10.64x trailing earnings; RSI 81. [ASSUMED] Engagement is more likely than rejection — PayPal retained Goldman and Evercore rather than dismissing the approach — and the 10.6x floor bounds the downside asymmetry. No add into RSI 81, no trim the session before the decision: the position was built for exactly this event.
rationale.XOM: New 6, limit 148.85 (~1% above the 147.36 close). [DATA] Closed above its 20-day high Friday on 19.0M shares vs a 15.16M two-week average — a participated breakout, not drift; 24.6x with a 2.80% yield; earnings ~7/31 (~9 ticks) is the sleeve's next dated event. [DATA] Note: RH's pre-7/01 XOM daily bars are still interpolated garbage upstream — this book's bars come from the repo's verified backfill merge, and the breakout signal is entirely post-7/01 data. [ASSUMED] Second name in the energy sleeve diversifies single-company risk while keeping the same regime exposure.
rationale.GOOGL: Hold 5 into Wednesday's print. [DATA] Reports 7/22 after close; consensus $2.90 EPS (+25.5%) on $116.9B revenue (+21.3%); closed 346.77, -15.1% off the May high, 27.0x, RSI 42. [ASSUMED] The sell-the-beat pattern (ASML, TSM, NFLX) hit names the market was positioned IN; GOOGL enters de-rated and under-owned after four flat weeks — the only earnings setup left in the book where a beat can actually surprise positioning. Sized at 5% because the pattern is 3-for-3 against earnings bets; this is the test case, not a conviction swing.

charts.title: TSM daily close — a $40.2B beat (7/16) sold into the semi rout
PYPL daily close vs the $60.50 Stripe/Advent bid — board meets 7/20

reflection.looking_back: The 7/15 report queued four orders that filled at the 7/16 open — ASML out, NFLX in at 5%, GOOGL in at 5%, BAC trimmed 12→10 — around a book betting on two 7/16 binary prints (TSM pre-open, NFLX after close). Both prints BEAT the exact metrics the thesis named: TSM revenue $40.20B vs the $39.3B consensus cited, NFLX operating margin 33.4% vs the 32.6% guide cited. Neither paid: TSM fell 419→398 in two sessions as Moonshot AI's model routed the semi complex, and NFLX sank 7.3% to a fresh 52-wk low on the H2 deceleration guide and a $1.53B FCF print vs $2.72B expected — the new 5% position ate the full drop on day one. PYPL, the prior tick's win, held its gain (56.56, +21% on entry) with the board meeting set for Monday.
worked: The underwriting discipline itself: both named metrics printed and printed BETTER — TSM $40.20B vs $39.3B consensus, NFLX 33.4% margin vs 32.6% guide. The research called the numbers right two-for-two; that part of the process is no longer the problem.

PYPL: positioned 7/14 pre-event at 46.72, still +21% with a +7.0% spread to the live $60.50 bid and the board decision now one session away — the anticipation-not-confirmation rule keeps being the book's only reliable alpha source.
BAC: a fresh 52-wk high (62.115) printed on Friday's -1% session at 14.1x — the post-beat hold call from 7/15 kept the book's best carry working.
REGN: +11.3% over 20 sessions at 16.5x, one of only a handful of universe names near its highs in the rout — the valuation-floor sleeve again.
missed: The regime call, which is the one that cost money: three straight tech beats (ASML 7/15, TSM 7/16, NFLX 7/16) were SOLD, and the thesis kept underwriting single-name events in a tape that had stopped paying for them. The NFLX entry was the worst expression — filled at the 7/16 open into an after-close print, correct on the metric, -7.3% by Friday's close.

TSM: held 8% through the print per plan, the beat came, and the position still sits -13.4% vs the 460.19 entry because the sector narrative (Moonshot AI capex fears) overwhelmed the company result — sector beta was the actual bet, and the thesis never priced it.

Energy: the 7/15 report DROPPED the unfilled CVX limit add on the soft-PPI disinflation read — 48 hours before oil respiked and CVX/XOM broke to new 20-day highs. The book's own 7/14 CVX entry was the right idea; the 7/15 de-emphasis of it was regime-chasing in the wrong direction.
reflection.adjustment: Trade the regime first, the event second: this tick rotates the two spent-event tech positions (TSM, NFLX — realized losses, no catalyst inside 15 ticks) into the sleeve the tape is actually paying (CVX to 10, new XOM 6), and keeps only event bets that are either non-earnings hard catalysts (PYPL board, Monday) or contrarian-positioned prints (GOOGL 7/22, de-rated, RSI 42, sized at 5% as the test of whether ANY earnings bet works in this tape). If GOOGL beats and gets sold too, the book stops playing earnings until the regime turns.
